recommendation: Require builder-spec-governance (spec-gate's Builder lens) to check, at the CR that introduces a new operation cluster, whether every in-code guard/throw on that cluster's happy path already carries a covering error-case scenario — rather than accepting happy-path-only scenarios at spec-gate and retrofitting error coverage as a later, separately-authored CR that mirrors an existing sibling precedent.
evidence: coverage-gap recurrence x2 in unit/lifecycle: the `clear` command's unresolvable-ref + no-known-pane error scenarios were added as a retrofit CR (predates this ledger shard); cr128 (github-128-unit-error-scenarios) then added the identical error-case pair for focus/nudge/read explicitly to 'mirror the two existing clear error scenarios exactly' — the guard (resolveTarget) already existed in shipped code for all four operations, so gate coverage lagged code by at least one full CR cycle each time.

cr128 plan brief states its own justification as 'builder-spec-governance requires every operation to cover its error cases' — i.e. the gap was recognized only after the fact, not caught when focus/nudge/read were originally specified.

recommendation: Codify a SessionAdapter design rule (candidate home: a cyberlegion-plugin governance or the adapter's own spec prose): any adapter operation that changes peer/session state must verify the observable effect actually took hold before reporting success, and fail loud rather than report false success when it cannot. Audit sibling adapter operations (focus, clear, and any future submit-shaped op) against this rule rather than waiting for each to independently rediscover the same fire-and-forget failure mode nudge just fixed.
evidence: 150-nudge-boot-race root cause: `unit nudge` was fire-and-forget — `SessionAdapter.send` ran one atomic text+Enter submit and reported success regardless of whether the harness actually consumed the Enter (boot-race swallow), so the ship sat idle at $0.00 while the caller believed it started. The design doc frames the fix as a general anchor: 'acts on the actual failure symptom, not a predicted precondition' — i.e. verify-the-effect-or-fail-loud, not send-and-assume.

the same mission closed a second, related gap as a side effect: the prior nudge scenarios had landed @frozen but UNBOUND (no test bound the CLI wiring) since their original freeze; cr150's mocked-exec tests were the first to genuinely bind them. No evidence this UNBOUND gap recurs elsewhere right now (verify-scenarios already surfaces UNBOUND to the impl-judge per prior doctrine work), so this is carried as supporting evidence here rather than a separate strategy entry — worth re-checking only if another long-lived-UNBOUND case surfaces.

recommendation: When a spec-gate freezes a new scenario that establishes more precise error-vocabulary for a failure mode an existing frozen sibling scenario (same file, same underlying guard/throw) already describes with looser or stale prose, the same gate should flag the sibling as a freeze-preserving-reconcile candidate — rather than leaving the mismatch to be caught incidentally by a later spec-judge and require a dedicated follow-up CR the next cycle. Consider adding this as an explicit builder-spec-governance or spec-judge check: for a touched failure-mode's error message, scan same-file frozen scenarios asserting the same underlying guard for prose consistency.
evidence: cause: prose-impl-contradiction. cr128 (github-128-unit-error-scenarios, PR #152) froze 6 new focus/nudge/read error scenarios using the accurate 'no unit is addressable under that ref' vocabulary the shipped resolveAgent/resolveTarget code and its tests already realized (`no agent addressable as "<ref>"`).

cross-ref: github-128-followup-reconcile-unresolvable-ref — the close+clear sibling scenarios in the SAME file, covering the SAME resolveAgent failure mode, still read 'no unit is registered under that id' — a mismatch the spec-judge only caught as a 'follow-up observation on PR #152' after cr128 merged, forcing a second, separately-gated CR (ADR-0021 freeze-preserving reference-rename) to reconcile prose that a same-gate sibling check could have flagged at cr128's own spec gate.

verified corpus-wide as of this scan: no further stale 'registered under' phrasing remains in packages/cyberlegion/.agents/spec or src on origin/main — the follow-up fully closed this instance. The registry/surface.feature 'unregistered session/caller' language is a distinct, correctly-scoped concept (identity-registration state, not ref-resolution) and is NOT drift. This entry targets the process gap (gate scope = own diff, not same-file siblings), not a currently-live corpus inconsistency.
